Empires & Puzzles Gongoth Review: The Astral Dwarf Contender

Gongoth has arrived to smash minions and wither opponents into submission. This Astral Dwarf packs a serious punch with his Fast mana speed and unique damage-scaling mechanics. Is he the purple hitter your roster has been waiting for, or just another face in the crowd? Read our in-depth analysis to find out.
Gongoth Review: The Anti-Minion Annihilator from the Stars

Gongoth is a 5-star Legendary Dark (Purple) hero from the Astral Dwarfs family. Released in May 2025, he quickly established himself as a premier damage dealer designed to punish the heavy minion and mega-minion meta that has dominated the game. As a Barbarian, he has the potential to inflict bleed damage, but his real threat lies in his Fast mana speed and a Special Skill that scales violently against summoner-heavy teams. While he lacks a costume, his base form is potent enough to make him a terrifying presence on both offense and defense. If you ever wanted a dwarf who looks like he could bench press a dragon while simultaneously insulting its mother, Gongoth is your guy.

Gongoth sports a classic "space dwarf" aesthetic, combining heavy, ornate armor with cosmic purple hues that scream "Astral Plane." His design features a magnificent, braided white beard adorned with glowing purple gems, and a helmet that looks both ceremonial and practical for skull-crushing. He wields a massive weapon that looks like a cross between a mace and a pickaxe, perfect for cracking open heavy armor or, in his case, smashing through minions. The art style fits perfectly within the Astral Dwarfs family, emphasizing sturdy builds and magical, glowing accents.

With a base Power of 1184, Gongoth sits comfortably in the upper echelon of modern stats, though slightly behind the bleeding edge of heroes released in late 2025 (like the 1230+ Power heroes from the Temple Summon). His Attack stat of 1229 is impressive, making him a heavy hitter, while his Health (2092) and Defense (1203) ensure he isn't made of glass. When compared to contemporaries, he holds his own very well. He is stats-heavy enough to survive the initial volley in wars and fast enough to retaliate before he goes down.

"Twilight Star Strike" is a devastating skill. Hitting three targets for 400% damage at Fast speed is already top-tier territory. Historically, hitting three enemies this hard was reserved for Average or Slow heroes, or those with significant drawbacks.
The secondary effects push him over the edge.
Reducing the target and nearby enemies' Attack by -350 is a massive survival tool. It cripples the enemy's ability to kill your team with slash attacks or special skills.

The +25% increased damage received is a solid "soft" defense down. However, the scaling mechanic (+10% per minion/mega minion up to +85%) is where he shines. Against a team protecting themselves with minions (like the Cookie family or other summoners), Gongoth turns their defense into a liability.
Fast. He charges in 8 tiles, or 7 with a level 29 mana troop/magic troop and emblem bonuses.
Base Hit: 1229 Attack x 400% = ~4916 raw damage value per target.
Total output: ~14,748 raw damage spread across three targets.
Max Condition (6+ minions on enemy team): +85% damage taken. This effectively nearly doubles the damage they take from subsequent hits.
-350 Attack is a flat reduction. Against a modern hero with ~1500 Attack, this is roughly a 23% reduction in their offensive power.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Astral Dwarf family bonus is excellent for disruption. A 50% chance (for 2+ heroes) to dispel a buff from all enemies at the end of the turn is a passive dispel that can ruin the enemy's rhythm without you doing anything.
More importantly for Gongoth, the innate resistance to mana reduction (up to 100% with 2+ heroes) makes him a hard counter to mana controllers like Fergus or Waterpipe. Gaining mana when resisting is the cherry on top.
Bypassing minions and mega minions ensures his 400% hit connects directly with the hero's health pool. This is vital for a finisher.
He takes less damage from minions, increasing his tankiness against summoner teams.
He never misses. This completely negates Blind effects (like from Drake Fong or Justice) and Dodge effects (like from Constance or Rochefort). He is reliable.
gives significantly more damage on his special skill for the first 6 turns. Since he is Fast, he is very likely to fire within this window, making his opening strike absolutely nuclear.
Upon release in May 2025, Gongoth was hailed as a "Fast speed monster" who broke the mold for hit-3 damage dealers. He was seen as a direct answer to the Mega Minion meta.

By December 2025, while new heroes like Devyani have slightly higher base stats, Gongoth's 400% multiplier at Fast speed remains incredibly competitive. The "ceiling" for fast splash damage hasn't moved much past him.

Gongoth remains a top-tier PvP hero. He is not just a relic; he is a staple in attacking war teams and a very dangerous flank on defense. His specific counter-mechanics (Anti-Minion, Anti-Dodge, Anti-Mana Control) ensure he will stay relevant even as raw stats creep higher.
Gongoth currently does not have a costume. His base version is strong enough to stand alone without needing a costume bonus to prop up his stats.
Maximizes the 400% damage multiplier and the bleed damage from the Barbarian talent.
Slightly lower HP pool makes him vulnerable to snipers.
Ensures he lives long enough to fire his skill, applying the crucial Wither and Damage Increase debuffs.
Sacrifices kill pressure on his primary hit.
path (Attack focus) is recommended. Gongoth's primary job is to kill or severely maim three enemies at once. The faster he kills, the less damage your team takes.
400% damage to three targets at Fast speed is elite.
Bypasses minions and scales damage based on their presence.
Perfect Accuracy means he ignores Dodge and Blind.
Wither reduces incoming damage, and the damage increase debuff sets up teammates for kills.
Immune to mana cuts (with family bonus), protecting his timing.
Like all Hit-3 heroes, he becomes less effective if the tank dies and the enemies are split (hitting wings only).
The Wither and Damage Increase debuffs can be cleansed by supports or specific passive abilities (like some Garrison Guards).
If not emblemed for defense, he can be taken out by a strong sniper before firing.
His best role. Use him to break stubborn defenses, especially those relying on minions or dodge.
A solid Flank. His Fast speed puts immediate pressure on attackers.
Excellent in Rush Attack (Very Fast) and Buff Booster (high damage).
Usable due to high attack and Wither (survival), though not a specialist.
Defense Down followed by Gongoth's massive hit and Becky's follow-up.
Using Gongoth alongside other minion counters to dismantle summoner teams like Waterpipe or the Cookie family.
He hates heroes who reflect Purple (Yellow Titans/Heroes with elemental reflect) or those who prevent status ailments (Aron, Hathor).
Be careful overlapping his "Damage Increase" debuff with other similar effects if they don't stack (e.g., Gastille), as they might overwrite each other rather than adding up.

If you pulled Gongoth early, congratulations. He is a carry hero. Focus on maxing him immediately. He will clear map stages easily with his multi-hit and keep your team alive with Wither. Don't worry too much about the minion scaling mechanic early on; 400% damage is enough to kill almost anything in the early game.

Gongoth is worth the Limit Break and Alpha Aethers. His "Special Boost" aether power synergizes perfectly with his Fast speed. In the current meta of heavy stats and damage reduction, having a hero who not only bypasses minions but uses them to amplify damage is a key tactical advantage. He remains a primary weapon against the annoying "Stall and Minion" defenses.
When released, the community was shocked by the 400% number on a Fast hero.
"A bit too strong for fast, don't you think? It should actually be Medium!"

was frequent, with players feeling Gongoth offered way more utility (Wither + Dmg Increase) without the drawback of Insanity.

Gongoth is a powerhouse. He represents the modern era of "Fast" heroes where speed does not necessitate a sacrifice in power. By combining high burst damage, defensive utility via Wither, and specific anti-meta mechanics (Anti-Minion/Anti-Dodge), he secures his place as one of the best Dark attackers in the game. He isn't just a blunt instrument; he is a tactical nuke.
An essential hero for any competitive roster.
Note: This review is based on the hero's state as of December 2025.
